what_it_does: Declares a seed relation type named fragment-of: a directed edge from unit A (the part) to unit B (the whole), with an explicit inverse (has_fragment) and topic-space proximity.
observations: The file stem matches the relation id 'fragment_of'
RELATION_TYPE is a single dictionary with id, label, directed, inverse, near, and desc fields
directed is True and inverse is 'has_fragment'
The docstring references runtime/relation_types.py and relation_types/AGENTS.md
No executable logic beyond the RELATION_TYPE constant
standouts: Explicit inverse field 'has_fragment' on a directed relation type
'near': 'topics' indicating proximity is computed in topic space
id exactly equals the file stem 'fragment_of'
notes: This file seeds the fragment-of relation type (part→whole) with an explicit has-fragment inverse and topic-space proximity.
questions: How is 'near': 'topics' interpreted by the runtime?
How and where is the inverse relation 'has_fragment' materialized?
What consumes this RELATION_TYPE dictionary and enforces its schema?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the file: it defines the fragment-of relation type with the documented directionality, inverse, and topic proximity.
intention_signals: It appears to register a reusable, semantically precise directed relation type that supports bidirectional traversal through a declared inverse and is grounded in topic similarity.
novelty: The explicit inverse field on a relation-type definition and the 'near' proximity-space key are distinctive for a declarative relation registry.
contribution: Provides the fragment-of relation type definition, including its directionality, declared inverse, and proximity space, for use by a relation-type registry or runtime.
summary_for_embedding: company/relation_types/fragment_of.py defines the seed relation type 'fragment_of' as a directed part-to-whole edge with inverse 'has_fragment' and topic-space proximity.
intention_for_embedding: To register a reusable directed relation type linking fragments to their wholes with an explicit inverse.
